role: Secretary of the Guardian Council (1992–present); former Chairman of Assembly of Experts
bio: Ahmad Jannati has been a member of the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) since its founding in 1980, and its secretary since 1992 — the longest-tenured figure in any constitutional body of the Islamic Republic. A founding member of the hardline [Haghani Seminary](/orgs/haghani-seminary) in Qom alongside [Mohammad-Taqi Mesbah Yazdi](/people/mohammad-taqi-mesbah-yazdi), he is among the most extreme voices in Iranian politics, repeatedly calling for the execution of political opponents and systematically disqualifying reformist candidates. He served concurrently as Chairman of the [Assembly of Experts](/orgs/assembly-of-experts). His continuation in office into his late nineties has been attributed to political calculation over capacity.
editorial_notes: Born 23 February 1927. Guardian Council member since 1980, secretary since 1992. One of the longest-serving officials in IRI history.
